items.final_wording: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
source.setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
source.exclusions: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

items.rationale: The exact NICE psychosocial-assessment section supports documenting onset or context, severity or impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, the person’s concerns or goals and clinician risk context without using a prediction score.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
source.setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
source.exclusions: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

items.rationale: The exact ED section supports measured vital data, focused physical and mental assessment, injury severity, appearance, immediate safety and safeguarding only when assessed; it does not predict future suicide.

items.final_wording: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
source.setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
source.exclusions: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

items.rationale: The exact safeguarding and discharge section supports recording clinician-decided disposition, clinician-entered plan, completed risk discussion, specialist input and arranged handover only when completed.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use
source.population: Adults and children presenting to emergency departments with mental illness, self-harm risk or mental-health crisis.
source.setting: Emergency mental-health triage, initial assessment, documentation, observation and liaison working.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting clinician-performed mental-health triage, current risk, agitation, capacity to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination, safeguarding and liaison referral. It does not predict future suicide or authorise detention, restraint or treatment in the UAE.

exact_location.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

items.rationale: The exact RCEM triage section independently supports documenting current distress, immediate safety, leaving risk, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status; no future-risk category is generated.
